content.text: added new component @registry/new-york/progresive-blur.tsx update docs and demo for it and run pnpm fix
Called the Read tool with the following input: {"filePath":"/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/progresive-blur.tsx"}

<path>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/progresive-blur.tsx</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: "use client";
2: 
3: type ProgressiveBlurProps = {
4:   className?: string;
5:   backgroundColor?: string;
6:   position?: "top" | "bottom";
7:   height?: string;
8:   blurAmount?: string;
9: };
10: 
11: const ProgressiveBlur = ({
12:   className = "",
13:   backgroundColor = "#f5f4f3",
14:   position = "top",
15:   height = "150px",
16:   blurAmount = "4px",
17: }: ProgressiveBlurProps) => {
18:   const isTop = position === "top";
19: 
20:   return (
21:     <div
22:       className={`pointer-events-none absolute left-0 w-full select-none ${className}`}
23:       style={{
24:         [isTop ? "top" : "bottom"]: 0,
25:         height,
26:         background: isTop
27:           ? `linear-gradient(to top, transparent, ${backgroundColor})`
28:           : `linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent, ${backgroundColor})`,
29:         maskImage: isTop
30:           ? `linear-gradient(to bottom, ${backgroundColor} 50%, transparent)`
31:           : `linear-gradient(to top, ${backgroundColor} 50%, transparent)`,
32:         WebkitBackdropFilter: `blur(${blurAmount})`,
33:         backdropFilter: `blur(${blurAmount})`,
34:         WebkitUserSelect: "none",
35:         userSelect: "none",
36:       }}
37:     />
38:   );
39: };
40: 
41: export { ProgressiveBlur };

(End of file - total 41 lines)
</content>

todos.content: Explore project structure and existing component patterns

input.prompt:
Explore the project structure to understand how components are organized. I need to understand:

1. The overall project structure - look at the root directory and key folders
2. How existing components in `registry/new-york/` are organized - list a few example files
3. How docs are structured - look in any `content/docs` or similar folder for existing component documentation
4. How demos are structured - look for demo files related to components
5. Look at a complete example of an existing component's doc file and demo file (pick any one component like "blur" or similar)

Return:
- The project directory structure overview
- A list of files in registry/new-york/
- The path pattern for doc files
- The path pattern for demo files
- Full contents of one example doc file
- Full contents of one example demo file
- Any config files that might need updating (like a registry config or component index)

result.output:
Based on my exploration of the project structure, here's a comprehensive overview: ## Project Structure Overview This is a **Next.js** project using **fumadocs-mdx** for documentation, with components organized in a registry system similar to shadcn/ui. ### Root Directory Structure ``` /home/himmel/personal/prsui/ â”śâ”€â”€ app/ # Next.js app directory â”śâ”€â”€ components/ # Site components (UI for docs site) â”śâ”€â”€ content/docs/ # Documentation content â”śâ”€â”€ registry/ # Component registry â”‚ â””â”€â”€ new-york/ # Components in "new-york" style â”śâ”€â”€ lib/ # Utility libraries â”śâ”€â”€ hooks/ # Custom hooks â”śâ”€â”€ public/ # Static assets â”śâ”€â”€ styles/ # Global styles â”śâ”€â”€ components.json # shadcn/ui configuration â”śâ”€â”€ registry.json # Registry configuration â””â”€â”€ source.config.ts # Fumadocs MDX configuration ``` --- ## Registry Component Files (`registry/new-york/`) ``` /home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/ â”śâ”€â”€ accordion.tsx # Component source â”śâ”€â”€ accordion-demo.tsx # Component demo â”śâ”€â”€ link.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ link-demo.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ progresive-blur.tsx # No demo file â”śâ”€â”€ smooth-input.tsx # No demo file (used directly in doc) â”śâ”€â”€ split-action.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ split-action-demo.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ tooltip.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ tooltip-demo.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ typewriter.tsx â””â”€â”€ typewriter-demo.tsx ``` --- ## Path Patterns ### Doc Files - **Location:** `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/{component-name}.mdx` - **Examples:** - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/accordion.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/link.mdx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/smooth-input.mdx` ### Demo Files - **Location:** `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/{component-name}-demo.tsx` - **Examples:** - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/accordion-demo.tsx` - `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/link-demo.tsx` ### Component Source Files - **Location:** `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/{component-name}.tsx` --- ## Example Doc File (Accordion) **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/accordion.mdx` ```mdx --- title: Accordion description: Animated accordion with spring physics, group morphing, and accessible ARIA attributes. --- import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/accordion-demo"; ## Installation If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command: Otherwise, use the full registry URL: ## Usage After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder: ```tsx import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion"; const items: AccordionItem[] = [ { id: "item-1", title: "First question", description: "Answer to the first question.", }, { id: "item-2", title: "Second question", description: "Answer to the second question.", }, ]; export function MyComponent() { return ; } ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | | --------------- | --------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | | `items` | `AccordionItem[]` | - | Array of accordion items | | `value` | `string \| null` | - | Controlled currently open item id | | `defaultValue` | `string \| null` | `null` | Initially open item id | | `onValueChange` | `(value: string \| null) => void` | - | Callback fired when the open item changes | | `collapsible` | `boolean` | `true` | When false, an open item cannot be closed by clicking | | `className` | `string` | - | Classes applied to the root wrapper | | `classNames` | `AccordionClassNames` | - | Override classes for individual sub-elements | ### AccordionItem | Field | Type | Description | | ------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- | | `id` | `string` | Unique identifier for the item | | `title` | `ReactNode` | Content shown in the trigger row | | `description` | `ReactNode` | Content revealed when the item is open | | `icon` | `ReactNode` | Optional icon displayed before the title | | `disabled` | `boolean` | Disables the item | ### AccordionClassNames | Key | Description | | ------------- | ---------------------------- | | `root` | Wrapper around all items | | `item` | Each accordion row container | | `trigger` | The clickable button row | | `icon` | The icon wrapper span | | `title` | The title text span | | `chevron` | The chevron rotation wrapper | | `content` | The animated content wrapper | | `description` | The description text div | ``` --- ## Example Demo File (Accordion) **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/accordion-demo.tsx` ```tsx "use client"; import { BookOpen, Bug, Info, LayoutGrid, Space, SunMoon } from "lucide-react"; import { Accordion } from "./accordion"; const items = [ { description: "This is a demo platform used to showcase features and test UI components during development.", icon: , id: "platform", title: "What is this platform?", }, { description: "Yes. You can switch between light and dark themes from the settings menu.", icon: , id: "mode", title: "Is there a dark mode?", }, { description: "The latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge are officially supported.", icon: , id: "support", title: "What browsers are supported?", }, { description: "This is a demo environment, so some sections intentionally use sample text and mock data.", icon: , id: "placeholder", title: "Why am I seeing placeholder content?", }, { description: "Check the documentation or contact the project maintainers for additional information.", icon: , id: "learn", title: "Where can I learn more?", }, { description: "Use the feedback form or contact the development team with steps to reproduce the issue.", icon: , id: "bug", title: "How do I report a bug?", }, ]; export const BouncyAccordionPreview = function BouncyAccordionPreview() { return (
); }; ``` --- ## Config Files That Need Updating ### 1. `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry.json` This file defines all components in the registry. Each component entry includes: - `name`: Component identifier - `type`: Usually `registry:ui` - `title`: Display name - `description`: Component description - `files`: Array of files to include - `dependencies`: Required npm packages ### 2. `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/meta.json` This file controls the navigation order of components in the docs: ```json { "title": "Components", "pages": [ "index", "smooth-input", "split-action", "tooltip", "accordion", "typewriter", "link" ] } ``` --- ## Summary of Component Creation Pattern To add a new component, you need to: 1. Create component source: `registry/new-york/{name}.tsx` 2. Create demo file: `registry/new-york/{name}-demo.tsx` (optional) 3. Create doc file: `content/docs/components/{name}.mdx` 4. Update `content/docs/components/meta.json` to add the page 5. Update `registry.json` to register the component

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: ---
2: title: Link
3: description: Animated link components with underline hover effects and customizable icons.
4: ---
5: 
6: import { LinkDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/link-demo";
7: 
8: <ComponentPreview name="link">
9:   <LinkDemo />
10: </ComponentPreview>
11: 
12: ## Installation
13: 
14: If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
15: 
16: <CodeBlockCommand
17:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
18:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
19:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
20:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
21: />
22: 
23: Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
24: 
25: <CodeBlockCommand
26:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
27:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
28:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
29:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
30: />
31: 
32: ## Usage
33: 
34: After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
35: 
36: ```tsx
37: import { Link01, Link02, Link03, Link04, Link05 } from "@/components/ui/link";
38: 
39: export function MyComponent() {
40:   return <Link01 href="https://example.com">Visit example</Link01>;
41: }
42: ```
43: 
44: ## Variants
45: 
46: | Variant  | Description                                                                  |
47: | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
48: | `Link01` | Underline animation from left to right on hover. External `<a>` tag.         |
49: | `Link02` | Underline animation from right to left on hover. External `<a>` tag.         |
50: | `Link03` | Underline animation from center outward on hover. External `<a>` tag.        |
51: | `Link04` | Full-width highlight with mix-blend-difference on hover. External `<a>` tag. |
52: | `Link05` | Left-to-right wipe with mix-blend-difference on hover. External `<a>` tag.   |
53: 
54: ## Props
55: 
56: All variants extend the native `<a>` attributes via the shared `LinkProps` type, plus two extras:
57: 
58: | Prop        | Type        | Default           | Description                                                                |
59: | ----------- | ----------- | ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
60: | `href`      | `string`    | -                 | The link URL (passed to the underlying `<a>`)                              |
61: | `children`  | `ReactNode` | -                 | Link content                                                               |
62: | `className` | `string`    | -                 | Additional CSS classes merged onto the root `<a>`                          |
63: | `icon`      | `ReactNode` | arrow SVG         | Custom icon node; replaces the default arrow (add your own hover classes)  |
64: | `target`    | `string`    | `_blank` (Link01) | All other native `<a>` props (`target`, `rel`, `onClick`, â€¦) are forwarded |
65: 
66: ```tsx
67: import type { LinkProps } from "@/components/ui/link";
68: 
69: // Bring your own target / rel / onClick, etc.
70: <Link02
71:   href="https://example.com"
72:   target="_self"
73:   rel="nofollow"
74:   icon={<SomeIcon className="size-4" />}
75: >
76:   Visit example
77: </Link02>;
78: ```

(End of file - total 78 lines)
</content>

--- title: Progressive Blur description: A progressive blur overlay that fades content at the top or bottom edges using backdrop-filter. --- import { ProgressiveBlurDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/progresive-blur-demo"; ## Installation If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command: Otherwise, use the full registry URL: ## Usage After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder: ```tsx import { ProgressiveBlur } from "@/components/ui/progresive-blur"; export function MyComponent() { return (
); } ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | | ----------------- | -------------------------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------- | | `className` | `string` | `""` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the root | | `backgroundColor` | `string` | `#f5f4f3` | Background color used for gradient and mask | | `position` | `"top" \| "bottom"` | `"top"` | Which edge to apply the blur to | | `height` | `string` | `"150px"` | Height of the blur overlay | | `blurAmount` | `string` | `"4px"` | Intensity of the backdrop blur |

content.text:
All done. Here's what was created/updated:

- **`registry/new-york/progresive-blur-demo.tsx`** - Demo component showing ProgressiveBlur on both top and bottom edges over a gradient background
- **`content/docs/components/progresive-blur.mdx`** - Documentation with installation, usage, and props table

content.text: The issue is the mask and background gradient aren't properly compositing to create a progressive blur. The mask should use `black`/`transparent` for opacity control, not the backgroundColor.

input.oldString:
style={{
        [isTop ? "top" : "bottom"]: 0,
        WebkitBackdropFilter: `blur(${blurAmount})`,
        WebkitUserSelect: "none",
        backdropFilter: `blur(${blurAmount})`,
        background: isTop
          ? `linear-gradient(to top, transparent, ${backgroundColor})`
          : `linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent, ${backgroundColor})`,
        height,
        maskImage: isTop
          ? `linear-gradient(to bottom, ${backgroundColor} 50%, transparent)`
          : `linear-gradient(to top, ${backgroundColor} 50%, transparent)`,
        userSelect: "none",
      }}
input.newString:
style={{
        [isTop ? "top" : "bottom"]: 0,
        WebkitBackdropFilter: `blur(${blurAmount})`,
        WebkitUserSelect: "none",
        backdropFilter: `blur(${blurAmount})`,
        background: backgroundColor,
        height,
        maskImage: isTop
          ? "linear-gradient(to bottom, black, transparent)"
          : "linear-gradient(to top, black, transparent)",
        userSelect: "none",
      }}

content.text:
Fixed. The problems were:

1. **Mask used `backgroundColor` instead of `black`** - Masks use black=visible, transparent=invisible. Using the hex color broke the gradient.
2. **Background was a gradient** - The background should be solid (the `backgroundColor`), since the mask itself handles the progressive fade.

Now the blur is solid at the edge and fades progressively to transparent as you move away from it.

content.text:
Done. Two changes:

- `ComponentPreview` now gets `className="!p-0"` to remove the wrapper's `p-12` padding
- Removed `max-w-lg` from the content div so text fills the full width

The demo now fully occupies the preview box edge-to-edge.
now demo is broken [Image 1] , blur is only happening on initial state, when user start scrolling, the blur stays at the initial position fixed and blur don't progress
The blur is using a static mask, so it doesn't respond to scroll. I need to make it scroll-aware so the blur intensity changes as content scrolls through.

content.text:
Done. The component now has scroll-responsive blur:

- Finds the nearest scrollable parent automatically
- **Top blur**: fully visible when scrolled to top, fades out as you scroll down
- **Bottom blur**: fully visible when scrolled to bottom, fades out as you scroll up
- Uses two layers: a `backdropFilter` blur layer with dynamic mask opacity, and a solid background layer with a static gradient mask
- Listens to scroll events passively and updates the mask in real-time
